evidence.scientific_clarity: Stacked bars clearly show 4-class proportions summing to 1 across 5 conditions; y-axis uses percent labels 0–100%; title, axis labels, and legend are all present and readable.
evidence.typography: Script sets no pdf.fonttype/svg.fonttype; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans fonts, not Type 42 editable text; DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default.
evidence.palette: Uses a Tableau-inspired 4-color set (#4E79A7, #F28E2B, #59A14F, #E15759) that is colorblind-distinguishable and harmonious; no green/red directional misuse since these are purely categorical classes.
evidence.layout_density: figsize (6,4) with bar_width=0.55 gives well-proportioned bars; tight_layout removes excess whitespace; y-range exactly 0–1; no label overlaps visible.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption correctly describes the figure and names specific findings (C dominates Cond2/3/5, D most prominent in Cond1, Cond4 most even); legend is inside the plot which may obscure data for extreme values, but acceptable here.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is saved but uses Type 3 bitmap fonts (no pdf.fonttype=42 set in script); text is not selectable/editable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: All four required files exist and are non-trivial: figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, gen_figure.py (plus bonus figure.svg).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption identifies all four classes, states bars sum to 100%, names specific dominant classes per condition, and calls out the most even distribution condition — solid specific finding.

overall_strengths: Clean, readable stacked-bar design with colorblind-safe Tableau palette, proper percent y-axis, and informative caption with specific numerical findings.
All required output files are present; figure layout is well-proportioned with no wasted whitespace or overlapping labels.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0) — fonts render as Type 3 bitmaps in the PDF, making text non-selectable and unsuitable for journal submission.
Uses matplotlib's default DejaVu Sans font instead of a publication-quality alternative (e.g. Helvetica, Arial, or a serif like Computer Modern).
